key_themes: Strong Q1 earnings beat with revenue up 9.9% and adjusted EPS surging 59%
Commercial aerospace recovery driving margin expansion to 26.9%
Reaffirmed 2026 guidance amid supply chain improvements
Defense and space segment weakness due to divestitures and softer demand
Analyst consensus hold with modest price target upside
summary: Hexcel delivered a robust Q1 2026 earnings beat, with revenue of $501.5 million surpassing estimates by nearly 3% and adjusted EPS of $0.59 exceeding forecasts by 34%, fueled by an 18.8% surge in commercial aerospace sales and significant margin expansion from operating leverage. The company reaffirmed its full-year 2026 guidance for $2.0-2.1 billion in sales and $2.10-2.30 adjusted EPS, signaling confidence in ongoing aerospace recovery. The biggest risk is potential lingering supply chain bottlenecks in the commercial sector and softer defense/space demand, though overall sentiment remains optimistic.
